lessons.title: Drawio Benchmark Gap
lessons.observation: The repaired run still omits the benchmark-expected drawio artifacts for Step 4, so artifact completeness remains below the otherwise corrected workflow quality.
lessons.root_cause: This project generated Python diagram sources and PNG renders, but not the drawio files expected by the benchmark script.
lessons.recommendation: Add drawio equivalents for the dependency and runtime diagrams or relax the benchmark to accept the existing Python/PNG artifacts as the canonical format.

lessons.title: Raw Key Vault Certificate Warning
lessons.observation: The new bootstrap Application Gateway certificate resource compiles and deploys, but Bicep emits a non-blocking BCP081 warning because published type metadata for `Microsoft.KeyVault/vaults/certificates` is unavailable.
lessons.root_cause: The Azure control-plane certificate child resource can be deployed, but the Bicep type system cannot fully validate it at authoring time.
lessons.recommendation: Keep the warning documented, and replace the raw child resource with a first-party typed module when Microsoft publishes one that works with the private Key Vault baseline.

lessons.title: Provider-Side Preview Still Missing
lessons.observation: The deployment summary is now honest and structurally correct, but it still lacks an Azure `what-if` capture, so actual provider-side create/modify counts are not yet proven.
lessons.root_cause: This repair cycle focused on artifact and template correctness, not a live deployment preview against Azure Resource Manager.
lessons.recommendation: Run `az deployment group what-if` against a correctly tagged resource group and attach the real preview output before treating the package as deployment-ready.

lessons.title: Template Portability Gap
lessons.observation: The corrected Bicep remains Contoso-specific in naming patterns, contact defaults, and certificate subject defaults, which limits reuse despite the improved governance contract.
lessons.root_cause: The E2E repair focused on making the current Contoso run correct first; full customer-neutral parameterization was left as a follow-up.
lessons.recommendation: Promote workload naming, contact defaults, and certificate subject values into explicit parameter-file inputs if this template will be reused beyond the Contoso scenario.

strengths: All mandatory E2E steps validate successfully after the repair cycle
Challenger reviews now exist for the critical steps and no must-fix findings remain
Live governance discovery replaced the earlier template-only baseline
The Bicep package now aligns with the live 9-tag governance contract and cumulative phase model
Deployment artifacts now describe validation-only evidence honestly instead of overstating Azure-side preview coverage
improvement_areas: Add drawio diagram artifacts alongside the existing Python diagram sources and PNG renders
Capture a real Azure what-if preview before treating the deployment summary as release-ready evidence
Parameterize customer-specific naming and certificate defaults if the Bicep package will be reused
Replace the raw Key Vault certificate child resource when a typed first-party module becomes available
